summary: A functional test failed because a pip install command returned exit code 1 after pip raised an OSError due to repeated HTTP 502 responses when trying to fetch a GitHub-hosted package, causing the test assertion to fail.
root_cause: pip failed to download https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/... due to repeated HTTP 502 responses (OSError), causing the install command to exit with code 1 and triggering the test assertion failure.

evidence.reason: Shows the specific test that failed and that the failure was due to a script returning a non-zero exit code.
Identifies the exact pip install command that was executed by the test.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Shows pip raised an OSError due to repeated HTTP 502 responses from GitHub while attempting the download.
Confirms the pip command exited with a non-zero return code, which the test framework treated as a failure.
Indicates the CI job failed because the test-suite exit status was non-zero.

suggested_fix: Rerun the CI job to confirm whether this was a transient GitHub/network outage. If failures recur, investigate runner network egress and proxy/load-balancer logs or switch the test to use a cached/local copy of the test package or add retries/backoff around the pip download.
